an6.83:1.1: “Monks, endowed with six qualities, a monk is incapable of attaining arahantship, the foremost state.
an6.83:1.3: “There is the case where a monk is without conviction, is shameless, is without compunction, is lazy, is undiscerning, and is concerned with his body & life.
an6.83:1.4: “Endowed with these six qualities, a monk is incapable of attaining arahantship, the foremost state.
an6.83:2.1: “Monks, endowed with six qualities, a monk is capable of attaining arahantship, the foremost state.
an6.83:2.3: “There is the case where a monk has conviction, has a sense of shame, has compunction, has his persistence aroused, is discerning, and is unconcerned with his body & life.
an6.83:2.4: “Endowed with these six qualities, a monk is capable of attaining arahantship, the foremost state.”
